title: if formulae fed & then within 30 mins breast is used it is still marked as re...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

if formulae fed & then within 30 mins breast is used it is still marked as red in red

---

**Category:** `bug` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Bug: Mixed feeding (formula then breast within 30 mins) incorrectly shows as red/overdue.
Affected area: Feeding tracker component — the 30-minute interval check logic in src/.
Current behaviour: When formula is given and then the breast is used within 30 minutes, the breast feeding session is still flagged as red (indicating the feed is overdue or too soon).
Expected behaviour: If a breast feeding session occurs within 30 mins after a formula feed, the system should correctly recognise it as a valid follow-up feed and NOT mark it red.
Edge cases: Formula→breast, breast→formula transitions; multiple feeding types in a short window; the 30-min threshold logic should distinguish between "too soon" and "appropriate follow-up".
Acceptance criteria: After a formula feed, a breast feed within 30 mins should show the correct colour status (green/normal), not red.
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Two fixes applied.
  1. getRecentFeed(): Changed condition so that when a specific type is passed, it only returns a recent feed if the types are compatible for merging. Same type always merges; breast L/R can merge with each other; formula/pumped/other types do NOT merge with breast types.
  2. stopFeedTimer(): Changed getRecentFeed(null) to getRecentFeed(feedTimer.type) so the merge check is type-aware.
Approach: Previously getRecentFeed(null) returned ANY recent feed regardless of type, so a formula feed within 30 mins would absorb a subsequent breast feed (merging it in without updating the type or sides array). The warning color logic then couldn't find a recent breast feed entry and kept showing red. Fix ensures cross-type feeds create new entries.
Trade-offs: Breast L → Breast R merging still works via the isMergeableBreast check.

title: is tummy time in quick logs relevant for newborn
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

is tummy time in quick logs relevant for newborn?

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Feature/UX issue: Tummy time appears in the Quick Logs section but is not developmentally relevant for newborns (0-4 weeks).
Affected area: Quick Logs component in src/ — the list of activities shown for newborns.
Current behaviour: Tummy time is shown as a quick log option regardless of the baby's age, including for newborns who cannot safely do supervised tummy time the same way older infants can.
Expected behaviour: Tummy time in quick logs should either be hidden for newborns (< ~4 weeks), shown with an age-appropriate note, or conditionally displayed based on the baby's age.
Edge cases: Age boundary definition (what counts as "newborn"?); babies born early vs full term; transition age when tummy time becomes relevant.
Acceptance criteria: Newborns (e.g. < 4 weeks old) should not see tummy time as a prominent quick log option, or it should be clearly marked as age-appropriate only for older infants.
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Suppressed tummy time warning colors for babies under 1 month.
  Changed tummy time quickLogWarnings threshold from (babyAgeMonths < 12) to (babyAgeMonths >= 1 && babyAgeMonths < 12).
Approach: The "never logged" danger fires immediately when no tummy time exists, causing alarm for brand-new parents. For newborns under 1 month the warning color is now suppressed. The tummy time quick log button remains visible and tappable for all 0-5 month babies (AAP recommends tummy time from day 1), but won't show a red/amber warning until the baby is 1+ month old.
Trade-offs: Newborns can still log tummy time; the button remains in the grid. Only the color warning badge is suppressed for < 1 month.

title: tummy time guide not available
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

tummy time guide not available

---

**Category:** `bug` `content`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Bug/missing content: The tummy time guide is not available in the app.
Affected area: Guides or content section in src/ — wherever other activity guides (e.g. feeding guide, sleep guide) are displayed.
Current behaviour: When the user navigates to or looks for a tummy time guide, it is missing or not accessible.
Expected behaviour: A tummy time guide should be available, covering: what tummy time is, recommended frequency/duration by age, safety tips, and when to start.
Edge cases: Guide should appear in the right section/screen; guide content should be age-appropriate; links from tummy time log entries to the guide should work.
Acceptance criteria: A tummy time guide page/section exists in the app with accurate, helpful content, and is reachable from the relevant UI area.
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/lib/constants/guides.ts: Added TUMMY_TIME_GUIDE constant with 5 sections (benefits, ageGuide, positions, tips, safety). Reuses existing MassageSection interface.
- src/tabs/GuideTab.tsx: Imported TUMMY_TIME_GUIDE; added Tummy Time Guide section between Activities and Remedies. Shows NOW badge for babies < 12 months. Follows the same card+section rendering pattern as the Baby Massage Guide.
Content covers: why tummy time matters, how much by age (newborn through 6+ months), 4 positions (floor, chest, lap, towel-supported), 5 practical tips, and 5 safety rules.
Trade-offs: Section is placed between Activities and Remedies as it fits the physical-development theme. Uses C.s (sky/blue) color to distinguish it visually.
